summary: Single targeted substring patch to WIN-061 detail_evidence resolves the H_eff-averaging-method carry-forward hole flagged in WIN-061.c4 H8 (also c3 H7). The existing "(using H_eff ≈ 3,400 km)" parenthetical is expanded to disclose (a) H_eff ≈ 3,400 km is the simple linear average of H(r) across the dome disc from r=0 to R=20,015 km, (b) area-weighted averaging gives H_eff ≈ 2,135 km → ~35 Hz (farther from 7.83 Hz) and pole-only gives the lowest possible frequency ~8.8 Hz (still above observed), and (c) a strict upper-bound argument — because H(r) peaks at H(0)=8,537 km, any positive-weight average satisfies H_eff ≤ 8,537 km, so f = c/(4·H_eff) ≥ 8.79 Hz > 7.83 Hz. This converts the cherry-pick defense from an inductive "we checked three methods" enumeration to a deductive closure that handles every possible averaging scheme in one inequality, strengthening over the curmudgeon-recommended text while preserving its three-method disclosure. Character delta: +490 (5138 → 5628). Before-string verified unique. ~22 Hz figure, 7.83 Hz observed baseline, and the subsequent "The model cannot explain the normal-day frequency" sentence preserved verbatim. TLDRs and detail_verdict_text not modified (already consistent). Scope strictly ISS-1454; ISS-1418 (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS timestamp-forward registration, major, still open) and the WIN-038/WIN-034 cross-WIN additions remain for separate follow-up EXPs.
